review_text: Friendly, helpful staff, park is close to town and restaurants,  lovely spot.
Great park. Will be back.

We were here Australia Day weekend 2024. (I have photos on the site feed from that stay) and again on the 3rd of October 2025
This time we had the deluxe ensuite powered site. So roomy and clean.
My dog enjoyed the open spaces nearby, as she was tethered at the caravan site.
Added some photos.

Clean park. Grass and handstand.
Pool.

A well maintained park with clean spacious sites. Well located, just a 10min walk to the main Street. A great place to use as a base to explore the Barossa.

No worries at all with this park, we'd be happy to stay again 👍🏼
Good place for family and kids friendly staff
My daughter enjoyed making new friends, en-suite was nice and clean and the shower was to die for!!  Great place to relax and spend time with your family!! ⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Plenty of sites and cabins, friendly and helpful staff, games room, camp kitchen and heated small pool.
Within walking distance of the Nuriootpa centre. Reasonable prices. Cheaper if you are a big 4 member.
Great park for family's and children. Welcoming staff. Neat and clean cabins and large powered sites for the cans

What a great park. The staff were friendly and helpful. The amenities, what can I say.
After travelling extensively throughout Australia, these would be the cleanest and best maintained that we've seen. The park itself is lovely with plenty of space and really well maintained. It is next to two sporting ovals and we had a lovely Saturday afternoon watching the cricket.
Great location for sampling everything that the Barossa has to offer.
Will definatly be coming back to stay and recommending to anyone heading that way.

We had a great stay in the Chardonnay pet friendly cabin. First time taking our dog away with us and this was an excellent experience for him and us. The cabin on arrival was very clean and presented well. The gated area gave us the opportunity to sit outside with our dog in-between the many walks we went on. There are lots of great places to walk your dog including the adjoining ovals, linear park and Coulthard reserve behind the caravan park. The bed was comfortable and the cabin had everything we needed for a relaxing stay. The pool was beautiful on a hot day. Very quiet caravan park with great amenities for all kinds of travellers.
Playgrounds were awesome! Great facilities. Grassed space had lots of room for us to have awning out

We had car trouble and were getting  towed to caravan park.  Desperately rang caravan park at 1 min to 5pm, phone was answered and I was booked in. Staff were very kind and made a stressful  situation so much easier. Park was so tidy, amenities clean and pool was very nice. Highly recommend  this park and ladies in office and cleaning staff are wonderful. Thank you!!!! 😊

Unsafe, uninhabitable accommodation – refusal to refund.
We booked and paid for accommodation at BIG4 in good faith. Upon arrival, the cabin was not fit for occupation, particularly given extreme heat and the presence of two young children.
The air-conditioning was not operating to a level that made the cabin habitable, and there was no visible evidence of current electrical test and tagging on appliances. These issues were raised immediately with management. After consultation, it was clear the issues could not be rectified within a reasonable timeframe.
Given the conditions, we had no practical or safe alternative but to leave and secure alternative accommodation at short notice and additional cost.
Despite this, management refused a refund. The written response we received from the Holiday Park Manager stated:
“There will be no refund for your cabin as the cabin was checked on your departure day with everything working fine in there. Have a nice day.”
This response is dismissive, inconsistent with our lived experience, and fails to acknowledge basic consumer and safety obligations. Under Australian Consumer Law, accommodation must be of acceptable quality and reasonably fit for purpose. A failure of essential services in extreme heat constitutes a major failure, entitling a consumer to a refund.
We are now escalating this matter to Consumer and Business Services (SA) and SafeWork SA for review of both consumer compliance and safety standards.
This was a deeply disappointing experience, not just because of the condition of the accommodation, but because of the unprofessional and legally flawed response from management when the issue was raised.
Until this is addressed appropriately, we cannot recommend staying here.
Spent an enjoyable 3 nights stay in the Grenache  cabin. It has a beautiful garden setting out front and it was close to the pool. The pool was clean and well maintained. The cabin was clean and tidy
Beautiful place. One of our favourite caravan parks. Pool size struggles to accommodate number of guests but otherwise brilliant

Only positive things to say about this place! My family and I hired a cabin (1 bunk, 1 double) and 2 powered campsites which we asked to be close to the unit which they did. Beautiful family friendly location. We were there for a week. Would stay again. Thank you guys for making our first trip to SA amazing. The right accommodation is everything.

I knew we were going to have a great stay the moment I walked into the office and was greeted by the friendly staff. We stayed on a powered Premium Ensuite site with an RV for two days. Perfect location in the Barossa Valley. Quiet location we will definitely be back. I highly recommend staying here!
Nice and spacious caravan park. Lots of drive through sites. Plenty of things for kids to do

Loved this park! We rarely do caravan parks for many, many reasons. This one was great. We had a huge lush-lawned site next to some bushlands eith thousands of Crimson Corellas, Little Corellas, some Kookaburras and other common birds.
The park was maybe 40% full, it was post Festive Period. The bathrooms were really, really nice, certainly the best showers we have had in any park. Very central to explore the Barossa Valley.
Thank you! Now we keep heading West, back home to Perth. We’ll see you again when we do our lap in a few years’ time.
Nice grassy sites, even in summer.
We had a great time
I have never been to this place and have no intention on doing so. But otherwise a great time had by all.
Loverly to hang out here!
Just signed in for another 2 years
A lovely park ,beautifully cared for and very spacious sites . You even get local shopping discount card. Highly recommend staying here in this lovely town.
In a good spot with clean facilities. Basically in the middle of the wine region.  Very pleasant and helpful staff.  A no hassle place.  Plenty of kids stuff and two footy ovals nearby.

This park is one of the best we have stayed at. The reception staff are so helpful and nice. The park is really neat and tidy and you can get a coffee, a haircut and dinner all without leaving the park. My husband recommends the hairdresser. If the soccer club is open go visit as the meal was simple but good. All in all a great park!

This park has many choices when it comes to sites and accommodation.  It's clean, great amenities, large sites, faces two ovals, town nature park and lake on the perimeter, great walking and cycling trails. We'll be back!
Nice park with grassed spaces, clean facilities, barbecues and nice staff.
We had a lovely stay at the caravan park. The facilities were very clean and the grass was very nicely kept

The staff are attentive and very nice, great professional check in,  very quick.
The park is very clean and the amenities are modern and clean. Good communication via text. This is third or fourth stay and our pick when we're in the Barossa Valley.

Website was easy to navigate, find the appropriate accommodation style & tourist park facilities/information.
Online booking was easy.
Prompt clear response from staff with payment/booking details.
Great communication day prior to arrival, also day of arrival advising when your site/cabin/acc is ready for check in.
Excellent communication with regards to mobile services available onsite at the tourist park, eg : food truck - Saturday night, coffee van - Sunday morning & mobile hairdresser.
Welcoming, happy, pleasant, helpful, knowledgeable & polite staff on arrival.  Big shout out to office staff member Tina 😊
Cabernet Cabin 4 clean, tidy, nothing broken or not working.  Well equipped with all you might need.
Didn’t get a chance to explore or check out other facilities within the Tourist Park.  But have confidence in saying they too would be of a high standard.
Highly recommend Big 4 Nuriootpa Tourist Park & would happily stay again.

Great place to pull up camp the park is well maintained and staff are very friendly when arriving or leaving the park.
The amenities are clean and cared for.
Will definitely be back again.
Park Grounds were well kept and the coffee van was a definite highlight. It was our first time camping in our Swift and was a great experience.
We had a great spot . They moved our friends near us . The park was clean and quiet considering the events that were on . Thanks  Great !

We stayed at the Barossa Tourist park for two nights in an en-suite caravan site.
From booking the site, then check-in on arrival, everything was so professional, warm and Friendly. The park is very clean, organised and Tidy. The en-suite was impeccably clean and the location of the park is great. A very easy walk into town to all the local facilities. We would definitely stay here again. 10/10 for us!!

Stayed on a powered camp site. The sites were small to medium sized with well kept grass. Food trucks and coffee van was also available and in a good area close to town. Bathroom facilities were clean and well kept.
Staff were so friendly and helpful even when I had booked the wrong dates! So convenient and well priced for the Barossa Valley. We'll be back.

Awesome park, wonderful friendly staff, perfectly clean great amenities and the best location with easy walk or bicycle ride to Nuriootpa for coffee or dinner, easy access to the Nuriootpa to Angaston rail trail and the premium grass sites are absolutely worth it for best spot in the park in my opinion.

First time at this CP and very impressed. Beautiful grass drive through site made set-up very easy. Very clean facilities and well maintained grounds, with plenty of surrounding parks and ovals for walking and playing. Highly recommend to anyone visiting the Barossa Valley.
It is a great location, with lots of fun amenities.

Without a doubt the best BIG4 caravan park I've stayed in!
The staff are so accommodating and they make a huge effort in making your stay amazing, food trucks, coffee vans and even a mobile hairdressing salon all in the park!
Deffinatly be returning :)
Awesome clean neat park
Great spot. Close to town. Neat and tidy grounds.
Great park if you intend touring around the lovely Barossa Valley

Great caravan park.
Staff were very friendly on arrival. Drive through sites made for an easy set up.
The amenities were clean..
A great park, clean tidy and nice people.
We are here in Barossa to attend a wedding and booked the Caravan park. It was huge. The reception staff was nice. The site was clean. The bathroom was clean.
Excellent facility, clean and well maintained.  Great central location.  We will definitely be back again.
We return at least a couple of times per year. The "what's on" information brochure and the discount vouchers are great. There's so much to interest us and to discover.
Great caravan park in beautiful surroundings
Big caravan park in Nuriootpa

What an amazing park.  We recently traveled from Sydney to the beautiful Barossa, where we stayed at Big 4 Barossa Park for four nights.  We had a large grassed site adjoining our friends site and they were very roomy, tranquil and quiet.  Plenty of room to park a large van and a couple of cars if you needed.  The facilities were amazing.  As others have said, the BBQ's were clean and had grease-proof paper to make clean up a breeze.  The toilets/showers were spotless.  The camp kitchen's had a television  and plenty of picnic tables and seating to eat in large numbers.  We had a small dog and the two adjoining sports fields were ideal to allow him to run.  There is also a fenced dog park immediately opposite the front gate and so we found ourselves across there with the other dogs at least once if not twice a day. The park put on a free wine tasting in one of the new amenity halls, which we attended and tasted some beautiful local wine.  We purchased an number of bottles, which they kindly shipped (..as most wineries will do..).  The park is positioned within an easy walk of town with cafe's, bakery's, pub, A.T.M.'s and a large fresh food store within a  ten minute walk.  The park is meticulously maintained.  This is one of the best parks that I have had the pleasure of staying at and although 1400 kms away... i'd really love to come back some day!
Large park. Lots of sites. Clean amenities. Friendly staff.
Great campground. Premium grass sites the win.
Great park. Reception staff were more than helpful. The park is neat as a pin. Grass is green and lush (we were in tents) fantastic park.
The staff are outstandingly helpful and the grounds and facilities are impeccably well kept. Can't talk this park up enough.

We stayed 4 nights on a grassy drive through site. Quiet location & well maintained. 2x amenities/laundry blocks & 2 dump points. Camp kitchen & BBQ’s clean & ready to use (love the baking paper provided for hotplate - saves cleaning). Great TV room with tables tennis & the pool is great too
Family friendly but still quite. Walking distance to the town centre. Clean facilities and very good customer service.

A good park.
Clean toilets, good showers.
Camp kitchen was a long walk to get 2.
Great place to stay, we had such a great time at this park. Very clean and tidy cabin, everything you need for a week away! Friendly staff, neat and tidy grounds.
Spacious and shady sites. Friendly staff and van park is well situated to explore the Barossa.
This place is our Go-to. We love it
Very friendly and helpful staff .park is clean and well maintained all you need for a great holiday short walk to main street and shopping centre.
3 Nights well spent
Friendly staff always helpful. A lovely park with nature walks, great playground and good variety of caravan sites to choose from.

We have stayed at Big4 Barossa Tourist Park many times and enjoy our stay each time.Location is great,next to the bush Park and walking distance to town.The staff are very helpful and go out of their way to help in any way they can,a little gem and we will return many times ,thank you,regards Geoff Faulkner.
The best caravan park I’ve been to. The cabins were so comfortable and clean and beautifully set up. The facilities are terrific. We’ll be back. Thank you

Had a fabulous week staying here with a premium ensure kids thought it was better than the bathroom at home! Will absolutely stay here again next time we visit. Close enough to Adelaide for a day trip. Had a wonderful meal from the Nuriootpa chicken shop that does amazing Indian cuisine yum yum yum
Great staff at the park ⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We love this place 🤩

Very friendly staff, they also let me check-in before 12, and its a short skip and hop into town for shops and amenities. But:
a) only 2 unpowered sites ($35), and both are on compact gravel. So much grass all around, but is either for powered sites ($50) or decorative.
b) wifi is fast, but capped at 1GB per day (used it to upload photos taken since my last CP)
c) wash and dryer is $5+$5, but the wash cycle is a bit short (not cleaning very well and a bit expensive). As a hiker, everything, except rain cloths, went into the washer, so air-drying isn't an option.
d) Amenities are off-limit from 9:30am, with 10am check-out. Would be a little more user-friendly if cleaning started after check-out. (there is a 2nd amenities block, but who has time to run around to different ones?)
We loved thus place. All you need here great sites. Clean amenities. Super town. Loved it.

Friendy staff, so helpful on arrival. Beautiful grassy site,  firepits (buy some wood at reception), clean amenities.
Some light road noise during day, but very quiet at night.
Lovely spot

Our first time here, but won’t be our last.  Sensational van sites and the cabins all looked very well maintained, including the ones with dog fencing, kennels and bowls!  Very well set up, pool, BBQ’s fire pits, recreation areas etc.  Even footy played a few metres away on weekends.  Extremely friendly and efficient staff.   A very well presented tourist park.  Well done.
Excellent will be back..

This is the 4th time we have stayed here. And while it's a good park we probably won't be back. We stayed on the premium sites this time. The sites are a good size with a nice firepit but the drainage when it rains is not great with the sites becoming water logged and very muddy. The town also goes into lockdown at 12.30pm Saturday with not even the bakery open Sundays.
I'd only recommend if you are really really into wineries. Otherwise there are better more interesting places to stay in the area.
Nice big sites, good amenities,  heated pool and great playground. Friendly staff.
Lovely park.

Powered grass site booked for 2 nights.
Easy check in. Friendly reception. Park clean and tidy. Amenities well maintained. Camp kitchen great space to use.
Would recommend 👌
Great for families. We are a couple but not overly noisy.
Unfortunately the information on things to do in caravan park is wrong,  says face painting but has wrong dates posted

Such a beautiful place to stay..staff are very friendly and accommodating, always clean and tidy, and the powered sites are roomy. Always so much to do, the walk into Angaston on the trail through the grapevines is just beautiful. Great shops in Nuriootpa which is a short walk from the park, and you even get a discount voucher for all the shops. Thanks for always having us.,👍👏🌟
Cool for the lids! We have had lots of fun here .
Good value.clean nice rooms and good crockery and cutlery.
Great park friendly staff
Nice large park with good amenities and pet friendly.

Lovely park, sites are tight and they had people park facing us, rather than awning to non door side. Then they ask if we have a dog, no, but put us in a part surrounded by people who have dogs. Shitting in our view as we are facing them and barking a lot. If people want to bring dogs,put them all together to annoy each other rather than non dog people.  Same with smokers. Put them together,away from non smokers. Speeding in the park is a major problem, a sign won't save a child's life, pulling them u will.
We stayed here over a long weekend,  the people around us and staff were friendly. Good facilities,  kids enjoyed the playground  and pool.
Loads of shaded sites. Great amenities and camp kitchen. Will definitely recommend and return. Easy check in and discount vouchers for local businesses.

Be aware if you have a site in the caravan park by the oval, the cricket club may hit a ball into your car smashing a window and ABSOLUTELY NO ONE will take responsibility for it, instead they will laugh at you and pass you around to each and every other person to avoid any responsibility. Disgraceful!! We won't be back and will never recommend this place after this

I've stayed here a few times, and I rate my discovery parks by how clean the bathrooms are, and this is why they get a 5 star from me. Clean well maintained, and the park is close to all the tourists' sights. Highly recommended Gerry (All About Adelaide Tours)
Great park never seen cleaner just spot on

Very friendly staff and clean, modern amenities. Very accommodating for my slightly out of the ordinary booking of a powered site when I was arriving by bike and camping ultra light. The soft grassed site was great for this purpose, able to lock my bike to the power pole and charge the e-bike. The camp kitchen was spacious and well equipped, toilet and shower block clean and modern, and the pool great for a post-ride swim. The whole park was quiet and friendly and perfectly located to explore the Barossa.
Lovely caravan park, very well facilitated, the premium sites are our go-to, some of the best in SA that we have experienced.

~ Bec ~ 🌸 you R the reason for the
........   ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐   ........
Your the reason I'd come back to the park...!!!
Thank you for EVERYTHING.
-- FROM --
- Pine trees, Pine cones and     Cockatoos.
- Lifting and Moving Mattresses.
- To guiding a blonde woman reverse her caravan.
- Even taking the time, to come say goodbye on your day off 💞..
You went ABOVE AND BEYOND in what you had to do. I will remember your kindness for a very long time.
~ Tara 🌸 I would also like to say thank you to you, for finding space for me and getting Tim to help me out as well...
____________________________
my opinion on the overall park:
• Toilet and showers Clean
• Laundry ok
• Pool and Grounds beautifully maintained
• Caravan sites very generous
in size and easy to drive through
(JUST DONT GET SITS 27,45,46)
--Because of the cockatoos, they will drop sap and heavy waterlogged pine cones onto your car n caravan and damage them. --
(PS hopefully someone passes this message on to whom ever is encharge of making decisions, because, I don't believe I'm the first person and I'm not going to be the last person who goes to their insurance company about damage to their property from those pine cones.
You need to look into REMOVING THE PINE TREES before someone gets hurt or something much worse happens.
My opinion only...!!

I’m rating 5 stars because overall the place was excellent but I wanted to ensure anyone seeking accessibility is aware of limitations of the accessible cabin. We stayed in the accessible cabin (though fortunately did not need specialised accessibility ourselves), but I happen to be an OT and was disappointed to find many aspects that would make the cabin extremely difficult, if not completely unsuitable for many who did need true accessibility.

- The front door was very difficult to lock and unlock, it did not stay open without a chair in front of it.
- the bathroom door lock requires good fine motor skills
- the shower has a frame around it, but perhaps a ramp and floor fitting is provided if you need? The tiles were extremely slippery when wet - we had to put the spare bath mat on the floor in the shower.
- the toilet was very difficult to flush - my children couldn’t do it.
- the space to access the toilet was also small and a wheelchair transfer would be a challenge.

Outside of the accessibility, the basin was missing the pop up plug so if you happened to drop something in there, you’d lose it.

The positives that earned 5 stars:
- the staff were the friendliest I’ve come across at all times - when I phoned to book, pay, on arrival, and in the encouragement of my youth children with the hunt around the park.
- the playground is fantastic (though weirdly high swings!). Good there is a more basic one as well that’s probably better for small children. And that one has a fence.
- the pool area was great, a nicely sized toddler area with some shade.
- the activity room near the pool was great - air conditioned with table tennis, foosball, tv, comfy couches and a big connect 4.
- the kids hunt was entertaining for my 5 year old and would be even better for slightly older kids and those with their bike to go off independently.
- there was a coffee van on site 2/3 mornings - hallelujah!

Great spot and decent value.

Wow what a fantastic stay! On arrival I honestly felt like I was checking into a retreat with the customer service from front desk.
The amenities are so clean, they have a great camp kitchen, pool and a great reserve to walk dogs.
Lovely park. Clean, tidy, great facilities and the staff were very friendly. Highly recommend

Ein sehr schöner Holiday Park mit schönen Plätzen unter vielen Bäumen. Man kann leicht zu Fuß die Einkaufsmöglichkeiten des Ortes erreichen. Insgesamt eine wunderschöne Umgebung. Wir haben hier die letzten 5 Tage unseres Urlaubs sehr genossen und werden wenn es uns möglich ist gerne wieder kommen.
Nice and relaxing, clean services and nice people. Kids loved the pool, the nearby 50 m memorial swimming pool is nice for more serious swimmers. The vibe is fantastic. Definitely recommend it.

The staff is professional and friendly. The pitches are rather small by Australian standards, but enough for a short stay.
The location is great as a base for exploring the Barossa Valley. The sanitary facilities are a dark chapter, they are dirty and there is nothing to gloss over, which does not justify the price in any way.
Loved it there, had a grass site and it was huge.

Awesome place to stay. Staff are so friendly. Also a big shout out to the female grounds keeper. Oh my god this person doesn’t stop. Just going hard out every day of the week. I hope she is being well looked after cause I haven’t seen anyone work the way she does in a long time. Absolute weapon. No wonder the park looks amazing

Great location, super friendly and helpful staff, well maintained grounds, spacious sites, spotless amenities, and only a short stroll to the shops and cafe's. Oh, and it's right in the middle of wine country!
The cabins are well appointed, comfortable and very clean. The park is beautiful with bbq area and pool
Nice clean park excellent staff, nice and quiet.

It is a beautiful park with wide, spacious lawned areas for caravans. The amenities are clean and updated. Dogs are welcome on leads. There are wonderful areas for children to play. Friendly staff are happy to find a solution for your requirements. A little pricey for one night but discounted for longer stays. A great location for exploring Nuriootpa SA and surrounding areas. This park is an excellent example of the brand Big4. 😀

Big park with everything you need. Right in the heart of the Barossa wine region. Slightly dated amenities but in great clean condition. Pet friendly options, also has pet friendly cabins with little fenced yards too

Been to Parks like these before the very nice we have a smaller cabin its very romie and warm. Has very good shower pressure.does have a small TV that makes it hard to see it. But it's pretty and nice.
Great caravan park , amenities were clean , water pressure ok   Park is lovely and green
A lovely Caravan park with clean amenities and the grounds very well kept.
lovely staff, nice grounds and amenities are good.  will stay again
Great park.. awesome big playground, a couple of mediocre small ones.. large grounds plenty of room.. Large kitchen to use and other dining rooms.. games for kids..  cute toddler/preschool pool.

Sites seem to be smaller than average. Handy location. Like the fact that nature is evident in a town caravan park, and I sleep well normally, but birds were singing throughout the night and did keep me awake. Happened all 3 nights. Not the fault of the park obviously, and perhaps common in the general area.
Watched football there but have been told great caravan park
The best and cleanest toilets and showers anywhere

Just spent 4 nights in our new van in the premium ensuite site (2)... We checked in & we're welcomed by friendly reception staff who were very helpful....Our premium ensuite was really nice & clean ,We enjoyed using the parks bbq and camp kitchen and overall it was a really great place to stay & we enjoyed our time away  ...
Would recommend this caravan park and cant wait to go back and stay again
Great staff and great park.  Nice and central to the Barrossa
Very clean park with excellent ensuite sites.

Staff here are incredible service the best and friendly nothing too hard
Would recommend for everyone passing by to stay a few days
Good amenities. Sites are level (looking at you Lake Bonney). Decent playground for the kids. Dog friendly.

On arrival, the staff are very friendly and knowledgeable about the area.
Park is kept very clean, amenities are kept clean also.
Camp kitchens, dump point.
Dogs allowed here, there is a nice walk behind the park.
We had a drive-through site, and they were quite big with power and water.
Would definitely stay here again.
And would recommend 👌

Lovely stay at Nuriootpa   Ammenities immaculate. The ladies at reception were extremely helpful and giving us some great ideas for site seeing. Enjoyed the walks around and behind the park. Lovely community gardens behind   Footy oval beside wasn’t noisy at all. Loved hearing a group of fellas play a game one night. Will certainly be back.

Very disappointed with the customer support. We booked a powered site at 9:30 am for that night which we need to pay up front. We had a traffic accident which changed our plans so I rang back at 12pm to advise that we could not stay that night. They said their policy was not to refund fees on the day of arrival even if they were not booked out.
I have been a Big 4 member in the past but I'm reluctant to use Big 4 ever again.
This is a huge park with amenities and facilities that are adequate but a little overpriced for the 'cattle class' experience that is delivered.
Great park. Facilities clean, close to town and close to bike trails. The park surrounds the local footy ovals though so it may be a bit noisy on game days.
Sites too close together
Went today to visit family. Very impressed with set up and cleanliness. Puppy even enjoyed it
Easy access to town and quiet area.

We have a 26 foot van and it was pretty tight.  We struggled to park the car on our site and could not get the awning out.  There are lots of trees which looks beautiful but the car and van were covered in leaves and we struggled to get the Starlink to work.  If you have a smaller van or motor home then defiantly stay - the facilities are very clean, the park is quiet and the grounds are beautiful.
Huge variety of sites to cater for all van / RV needs, clean facilities and lots of room / activities for the kids.

Nice spacious park, with lots of large trees providing shade. 3 and a halfish stars, but not quite 4. Could do with a slightly bigger pool considering the number of patrons when park is full. Amenities could be better maintained, eg toilet paper, to be replaced more often.

The place is amazing however the ants are not. The beds don't get changed daily and when you ask for them to be made the cleaners say they are made when you leave. While it's very peaceful and you have a personal pool, customer service lacks in many ways and rules only apply to those that the person turns a blind eye to.
Camp-site very good and clean will go back again
Our go to place ♥️

The sites were sizable and the park layout is great, but our grass site was primarily mud which meant with the rain over the weekend that it was a bit messy. Being a long weekend, it could have also been good to have some extra maintenance of the bathrooms as when I went in there over the four days there was often no toilet paper or paper towel, and the floor and sink area was quite dirty.

The playgrounds and pool area was lovely and amazing play selections for the kids, especially with the ovals right there, however there were maintainance staff mowing the ovel at 5.00am on our last morning, which woke our children and several other children at sites near by. There is also a fair bit of traffic noise from trucks from 3.30am onwards, but being vintage season I think that's to be expected.

Stayed here recently for a night whilst exploring the Barossa, the facilities and park were okay however not worth the $70 for a campsite for one night? That is very excessive when we were not using any power simply setting up a rooftop and using our own battery set up. I have never paid anymore than $30 for an unpowered site, you’re simply paying for the location.

Good park although it was PACKED due to NYE. Sites aren't very big so you are pretty close to your neighbours. Pool isn't very big but the kids still liked it. We had an ensuite site which was clean and shower was hot. Loads of dogs which I liked however if your not a dog person then maybe get a cabin where they aren't allowed.

Fantastic location and facilities! The maintenance and office staff are always fantastic👏 we’ve stayed a few times and on the last couple we’ve been lucky enough to get the larger premium grass sites. Last time the bore water strainer failed and lightly flooded and maintenance and office staff were fantastic!! Can’t recommend highly enough. Everything is so well cared for and clean (Yes the amenities were empty for pictures 🤣).
Friendly & helpful staff great park cabins spotless & very comfortable
baylin crouch bowls pies

Worst experience every. Rude pedantic  staff. Made wait 2 hrs wile site was watered and made ready. On arrival at site,it was covered in rubbish, site not watered as advised was happening and found a car parked on site wich I had to wait 1hr for car owner to arrive before I could park and set up my van. Other occupants  of sites also complained about service.
4 wonderful days.
Very pretty spot, best one I’ve stayed at.
Well laid out, good location with sports ovals on one side and a park on the other. Quiet area and a short walk to shops and pubs.

Had a very enjoyable 4 day stay,  the park is big and spacious with everything you need for a great stay. If your into AFL I can't think of a better place to stay for the gather round
, as your right there.   We will return
Beautiful campsite. Lots of fun things for kids (3 playgrounds!) And adults too! (Lots of wineries close by!).

We had a fantastic 4 night stay. The staff could not have been friendlier or more accommodating. The family cabin accomodation was clean but basic. If you have kids the pool, the well equipped games room (with tables tennis and giant connect 4) and playground are what makes it an easy stay. The grounds are well maintained and laundry facilities easy to use (bring  gold coins). The location was excellent 10 mins to local wineries and attractions. Will definitely be back.

Great spot to park up and venture out to wineries in the area. There are heaps to choose from! Plus antique shops, gardening, trinkets, breweries, chocolate shop so much to see and do in this whole area. Worth staying a whole week.
Had a sisters catchup on the Friday with a one night sleepover. Cabin was very nice, spacious, plenty of storage, clean, beds were very comfy, staff friendly also 👍

We stayed in a cabin at Big 4 Nuriootpa in April as part of  a group and we had a fabulous time.   Great amenities for all ages and we  can't wait to go back again.
It is a fabulous venue!
Staff are great!

Had a great four days here exploring all that the Barossa Valley has to offer. We will definitely return, in the not too distant future.
We had the Shiraz 2 cabin, very spacious, clean an everything you could possibly need

Awesome big site, kids loved the playground and pool.
Really nice and green, mature trees everywhere. We really enjoyed this park.
Lovely park close to town with large AFL ovals next to it.
A great park, friendly staff, the cabin was bright, clean and spacious. We’ll certainly come back and stay longer next time.

Staying in the Merlot2 cabin tonight and it's a great cabin and immaculately clean! Great BBQ area and loved the baking paper provided for lining the bbq plate.
Well looked after park. 😊

A basic but clean and tidy cabin- small, annoying things that didn’t make things unbearable but we’re definitely inconvenient-
First cabin had no power, we were quickly moved but luckily hadn’t unpacked much
Bathroom door handle broke meaning the door couldn’t be fully closed then the smoke alarm would sound every time the shower was used and steam was escaping
Toaster not working
Bare minimum cutlery and crockery- everything so small! Tiny saucepan, tiny bowls, tiny spoons!
Window rattling heater- would have been nice to be warm overnight but too loud to sleep with it on!
Not the parks fault but the other patrons weren’t great- lots of loud noise at night, people completely taking over BBQ and fire pit areas and not budging for anyone else, cars constantly speeding through, people parking all over the place haphazardly and blocking roads, I was the only parent supervising my own kids with about 30 other kids at the playground. I don’t know how you’d managed to enforce rules with some people but I think something more could be done.
Great park for all families and all ages

Awesome! We LOVE LOVE LOVE Big4 holiday parks! Stayed for 4 nights in May 2023 in a Jay cabin. Spotlessly clean, very well equipped cabin. Check in was a breeze. Front desk staff were helpful, friendly and  email communication was swift. Strategic location. No doubt about it, we will definitely stay here the next time we visit Barossa. Highly recommended! Thank you so much Big4 Barossa for making my stay a memorable one.

Grassed site, short walk to amenities.  Amenities nice and clean.
Love the featured entry showcasing first caravan built in the 1920s by Mr Kaesler.  Plenty of trees for shade if required.

Great location and customer service. Nice neat cabin but the old style window rattler heater was so loud it made it impossible to sleep when it was running so my heavily pregnant wife and I had to choose between sleeping or freezing in 5 degree temperatures. A split system unit would make for a much more enjoyable stay.
